of politics because it's poisoning Washington.
I approve this message because above all, we must put country first.

00819.text: Telling endless lies about Claudia Tenney on Spectrum and Healthcare, Anthony Brindisi can't run on his record. In Albany, Brindisi rubber-stamped Cuomo's New York City agenda. In Congress, Brindisi would work for Pelosi, attacking the Second Amendment, giving amnesty to illegal aliens, even impeaching President Trump. Liberal Anthony Brindisi is a Cuomo-Pelosi puppet. Claudia Tenney fights for us, growing our economy, protecting seniors' healthcare, defending our constitutional rights, and standing with President Trump. I'm Claudia Tenney, and I approve this message.

00820.text: I'm Claudia Tenney and I approve this message. Fighting for what's right isn't easy. Holding powerful politicians accountable has earned me enemies in politics. I stood against Cuomo's corruption and was the first to demand the resignation of convicted Speaker Sheldon Silver. That's why corrupt politicians are attacking me, running false ads, hoping to replace me with one of their own. Republican or Democrat, it doesn't matter. I'll never stop fighting to make sure government is serving the people, not the powerful. Thank you.

00821.text: America's comeback is in full swing. Jobs, opportunity, hope. But Anthony Brindisi wants to rewind the clock, siding with Pelosi, supporting policies that hiked our taxes and sent jobs to China. Tenney and Trump fight for us. Through tax cuts and real reform, we fought to unleash the American dream. Jobs are coming back, our economy is booming, and families are seeing real results. I'm Claudia Tenney and I approve this message because America's comeback is just getting started and we can't risk going back.

00834.text: It's never been more clear. Elections matter. Here's what's at stake in this election. Whether every Montanan has affordable health care, whether we protect Social Security and Medicare, whether we have leaders who are role models for our children. On every one of these issues, Greg Gianforte and I could not be more different. Montana deserves a representative who will be there for all of us. I'm Kathleen Williams and I approve this message.

00835.text: Over a century ago, Montana led America by electing Jeanette Rankin as the first female member of Congress. This year we can lead the way again. We can choose empathy over anger, civility over violence, and leadership over partisanship. We can stand up for those who have no one to believe in them, believe in those who have no reason to lie, and invest in our future, not mortgage it. And we can finally give Jeanette Rankin a long-overdue successor. I'm Kathleen Williams and I approve this message.

I approve this message because you don't often see 20 offices or a plan with lots of details. But we're changing that.

It's time for real change, so it's time for Rodney Davis to go. I'll see you next time. Bye.

00921.text: I have a heart condition that's considered a pre-existing condition. At my congressman's one and only public event of 2017, he said he was only going to support a version of Obamacare repeal that let people with pre-existing conditions keep their health care coverage. Randy Holkren voted against people like me with pre-existing conditions, and I decided, you know what? It's on. I'm running. I'm Lauren Underwood, and I approve this message.

00922.text: As a registered nurse treating patients, a health care policy advisor, and especially as a woman with a pre-existing heart condition, I've seen the cost of health care soar and millions of Americans suffer. I'm running for Congress because every family has a right to quality, affordable health care. We must work to expand coverage and make affordable health care a guarantee. No American should have to make a choice between their health and paying the electric bill. I'm Lauren Underwood, and I approve this message. Thank you.

00923.text: We almost lost our son Jack, but after 21 days in intensive care, great health care saved his life. I'm Betsy Dirksen-Londrigan, and I approve this message because on health care, I'll always put families first. We'll be right back.

I'm Cindy Axne, and I approve this message. I approve this message.

00952.text: June 15, 2008 was the last day I saw or heard from my son. The last time I saw my son, he was five years old. He's now 15. I need to see my son again. Mimi Walters has been a great champion for our cause, for abducted children. Mimi Walters has never stopped fighting for me to be able to see my son again. I'm Mimi Walters and I approve this message. I'll see you next time.

00958.text: I'm Jessica Morse. My ancestor invented the Morse Code as a way for people to communicate. Right now Congress could sure use a better way of communicating. That's why I'm running. I've worked with both Democratic and Republican presidents and I've seen what can be accomplished when partisanship is put aside. We need a new Morse Code to get Washington moving again. I'll work with anyone no matter their party to lower health care and prescription drug costs, to build our economy, and to fight for our community. That's my code. I'm Jessica Morse and I approve this message.

00959.text: Growing up, my dad was a Democrat and my mom was a Republican. We didn't shy away from disagreements, but we were always respectful. And that's what's missing in Washington today. We need more of that when we understand there's a cause bigger than ourselves. That's when America is great. I'm Ann Kirkpatrick and I approve this message.

Career politicians are the problem, not the solution.
It's time to drain the swamp.
The Constitution was written to protect our God-given rights and restrain government.
When taxes are low, small business owners are allowed to create jobs.
As a mother of a child who had special needs at birth,
I will always protect and defend the sanctity of life.
I'm Katie Arrington, and I approve this message.
Thank you.

I'm Dana Balter and I approve this message because Donald Trump is the biggest bully of them all. We have to stand together and stop him. Dana Balter for Congress, endorsed by every local Democratic committee.

01077.text: It's cancer. It's going to come back. That's what my doctor told me in 2009. There was a clinical trial that could help, but my insurance company wouldn't let me do it. Kathleen Williams was my state legislator. She listened to me, and she said, I'll dig into it. And she brought together doctors, insurers, patients, and wrote a law that required insurance companies to cover these life-saving treatments. No grandstanding, just results. Kathleen Williams gets things done, and I wouldn't be here today without her. I am Kathleen Williams, and I approve this message. Thank you.

01083.text: We were in a tough spot. Our battalion was cut off, we were under mortar fire, and a large group of the enemy was about to attack. That's when the call came in, and we were low on fuel. I'll never forget Amy on the radio. Cool, calm. But only enough time for one bomb. And Amy shacked it. It took out the enemy compound and turned the tide for us. Marines know we're all on the same team. I'm Amy McGrath, and I approve this message because Congress is broken. We need a new generation to fix it.

I'm Karen Handel, and I approve this message. I'll see you next time.

In Congress, I'll fight for Georgia's families and businesses. I'm Karen Handel and I approve this message because I'll do more than talk. I'll deliver.

Mimi Walters wrote a bill to help prepare and protect firefighters and provide the manpower and training we need to keep the citizens of Orange County safe. Mimi Walters will help us protect you. I'm Mimi Walters and I approve this message.

I knew then that Young Kim is exactly who we need representing us. Thank you.

01115.text: In 10 years, Tom McClintock has never voted for himself because he doesn't live in our district. But he votes in Congress, where he's voted against funding to prevent wildfires or to help homeowners afford to rebuild after natural disasters. It's easier to vote against the interests of your district when the people aren't your neighbors. My family's been here for five generations, and I want to help protect and defend our incredible wilderness, our water, and the families who live and work here. I'm Jessica Morse, and I approve this message. Thank you.

01116.text: I grew up exploring nearly every part of these mountains. I'm not afraid of a challenge and neither were my ancestors, like my great-grandma Edith, who manned the telegraph booth at Donner Pass with her pistol by her side. But generations later, we have real challenges in the Sierra, like protecting our watersheds and proactively working to prevent wildfires. I'm Jessica Morse and I approve this message because I'm not afraid to tackle these challenges in Congress. Thank you.

01381.text: I grew up in a big family, three generations under one roof. Mom and Dad were there for all of us, and as they aged, Medicare was there for them. It was a guarantee. As a voice for the Tea Party, Congressman Frank Ginta voted to gut the program. This helped our families for almost 50 years. My opponent would replace Medicare's guarantee with vouchers that will cost seniors thousands more. I'm Carol Shea Porter, and I approve this message because Medicare is a promise we must keep. I'll see you next time.

Soy Dina Tidus y apruebo este mensaje porque la educación es la clave del éxito.
Votemos por Dina Tidus en estas elecciones.
Dina Tidus

Determined to secure our ports. Fund new communications equipment for local police and first responders. And put America on a path to energy independence. Finally, a representative in Washington who represents us. Allison Schwartz. Congress.

01401.text: My grandmother was remarkable. A machinist in World War II. She taught me so much. She'd be heartbroken how the Republican extremists in Congress treat women. Opposing equal pay, denying access to family planning, my grandmother lived to 102. She saw a century of progress. I won't let them turn the clock back. I'm Katherine Clark and I approve this message. My grandmother would too.

01402.text: Women's issues are family issues. When women get short-changed at work, when we're not allowed to make decisions about our own bodies, when we can't afford to send our kids to college and our parents can't count on Social Security, it hurts everyone. The Republican extremists pay lip service to family values. I'll actually fight for families. These are our three sons. They'll do better in an America where everyone does better. I'm Katherine Clark and I approve this message.

01403.text: When I was in high school, I took an aptitude test and I scored off the charts in engineering. My advisor told me that because I was female, I should look for another field. Some things barely change. Today, the Republicans in Congress oppose equal pay for equal work, deny birth control. These extremists don't believe women's issues are family issues, but you and I know they are. I'm Katherine Clark and I approve this message. And so do I.

01404.text: When her father lost his job, Tammy Duckworth's family lost everything and had to rely on food stamps to get by. But she waitressed her way through college with the same perseverance that fueled her recovery after her Black Hawk was shot down in Iraq. Tammy went to work for President Obama, finding jobs for veterans in a tough economy. She'll fight extremism to protect Medicare and end tax breaks for shipping jobs overseas. I'm Tammy Duckworth and I approve this message. Because a little perseverance goes a long way.

01405.text: I was very concerned about the path that President Obama had our country on. I talk to small business owners every day. The uncertainty that they're faced with, not knowing what's coming, not knowing if their taxes are going to go up, not knowing if more regulation is going to tie their hands. They need to be given some certainty so that they can be investing in their own businesses, so that they can be creating jobs. This is what we need to do so that we can turn this economy around for our children. I'm Renee Elmers, and I approve this message. Thank you.

01415.text: I'm Ileana Ross-Lehtinen and I approve this message because I never forget who I work for. America is running out of time to meet the challenges that concern us all. I continue my work to strengthen the economy, to create jobs, lower taxes, help small businesses, and fight government waste. It's time for proven leadership. It's time to fight for you.

01421.text: I'm Ileana Ross-Lehtinen. Some forget who they work for, but I don't. That's why I supported the heart of our economy, small businesses, in their quest for access to affordable health care, and sponsored measures to increase services to those on Medicare. I supported legislation that creates incentives for the development of alternate fuel sources and promotes the use of clean and renewable fuels. I'm Ileana Ross-Lehtinen, and I approve this message because I'm honored to be working for you. Thank you.

01422.text: Confused by Bobby Bright, he says he'll oppose Nancy Pelosi as Speaker, but he voted for her once and doesn't regret it, and takes thousands from her to fund his campaign. Bright runs ads against Obamacare, but now says, It's doing some good things for 80% of the people that live in Alabama. You can trust Martha Roby. She'll change course and oppose Nancy Pelosi on taxes and spending, and get America back on track. I'm Martha Roby, and I approve this message.

01423.text: Exploding deficits, new and higher taxes, government-run health care, secret deals. That's how Washington works. My roadmap for Alabama is real conservative change. A plan to reduce spending, keep taxes low, and for common sense health care reform. All out in the open so you have a say. Nancy Pelosi won't allow this, but I'll vote to take away her gavel so we can get America back on track. I'm Martha Roby, and I approve this message.

segments.text: Exploding deficits, new and higher taxes, government-run health care, secret deals.
That's how Washington works.
My roadmap for Alabama is real conservative change.
A plan to reduce spending, keep taxes low, and for common sense health care reform.
All out in the open so you have a say.
Nancy Pelosi won't allow this, but I'll vote to take away her gavel so we can get America back on track.
I'm Martha Roby, and I approve this message.

01424.text: Behind closed doors, just a handful of congressional leaders meet in secret to transform America. They've exploded the deficit, passed massive taxes, and taken over health care. In Congress, I'll vote to reduce spending and say no to new taxes, just like I did on the Montgomery City Council. But my first vote will take the gavel away from Nancy Pelosi, so she will no longer decide your future and shut you out. I'm Martha Roby, and I approve this message.

But my first vote will take the gavel away from Nancy Pelosi, so she will no longer decide your future and shut you out.
I'm Martha Roby, and I approve this message.

01425.text: These are our children, Margaret and George. They already owe $51,000 and counting, their share of the national debt. Under Barack Obama, America has borrowed $5 trillion. My kids and yours will get the bill. So I voted to cut spending, cap it in the future, and balance the budget. Less government means more opportunity for small business to create American jobs. I'm Martha Roby and I approve this message because their generation is worth fighting for.

segments.text: These are our children, Margaret and George.

Under Barack Obama, America has borrowed $5 trillion.
My kids and yours will get the bill.
So I voted to cut spending, cap it in the future, and balance the budget.
Less government means more opportunity for small business to create American jobs.
I'm Martha Roby and I approve this message because their generation is worth fighting for.

01426.text: We all know people who have lost jobs in this tough economy. And Washington's making it worse by raising taxes and spending money we don't have. And now they're talking about a new energy tax on everything from gasoline to electricity? That'll kill even more jobs. Bobby Bright may be a good man, but as long as he's in Congress, Nancy Pelosi will be in charge. And together they'll force us down a road we can't afford. I'm Martha Roby and I approve this message.

01427.text: I'm just getting started. I don't quit. That just means more of the same. Higher taxes, massive spending, more unemployment. If you like this, then Bobbi Bright is your candidate. But if you want to check on the Obama-Pelosi agenda, if you want less spending, lower taxes, and a new health care law, if you want to get America back to work, then I ask for your vote. I'm Martha Roby, and I approve this message.

01428.text: This is the new health care law. It costs too much and explodes the deficit. A thousand pages of rules and regulations, massive new taxes, and a half trillion dollars in Medicare cuts. Here are two things Bobbi Bright won't promise you, but I will. I'll vote to repeal and replace this law, and my first vote will take away the gavel from Nancy Pelosi so she can't block us from changing it. I'm Martha Roby and I approve this message.

01429.text: Uncommon Leadership. Shelly Pingree. A state senator who took on the special interest to lower drug costs for Maine's middle class. A small business owner creating jobs. A commitment to community and family. A national leader on ethics reform as president of Common Cause. Now Shelly Pingree is running for Congress to finally end the war, reform health care, and create the quality jobs we need to move forward. I'm Shelly Pingree and I approve this message.

segments.text: Uncommon Leadership.
Shelly Pingree.
A state senator who took on the special interest to lower drug costs for Maine's middle class.
A small business owner creating jobs.
A commitment to community and family.
A national leader on ethics reform as president of Common Cause.
Now Shelly Pingree is running for Congress to finally end the war, reform health care,
and create the quality jobs we need to move forward.
I'm Shelly Pingree and I approve this message.

01430.text: Every major newspaper has endorsed Shelly Pingree for Congress. Calling Shelly a forceful voice, a good listener, and optimistic. The small business owner whose thoughtful, grounded experience will serve Maine well. Pingree was also a respected state senator whose record working effectively with both Democrats and Republicans gives her the experience and vision to reform health care and get our economy back on track. Shelly Pingree. For change. For Congress. I'm Shelly Pingree and I approve this message.

01431.text: A Democrat we can trust. Shelly Pingree, a single mom and state senator who took on the special interests to lower medicine costs for Maine's middle class. Now Shelly's endorsed for Congress by environmental groups for her plans on alternative energy, by Planned Parenthood to protect a woman's right to choose, and by respected labor groups to raise the minimum wage, end the war in Iraq, and start investing in jobs here at home. I'm Shelly Pingree and I approve this message. We're going to ask them what she wants. You're welcome. Thank you.

01432.text: Middle class families are paying the price for Washington's mistakes. Wall Street gets bailouts, while we're stuck with record fuel prices, lost jobs, and rising health care costs. In the state senate, I stood up to the special interests in big drug companies to fight for working families. In Washington, I'll do the same, ending the corporate tax giveaways so we can start investing in main businesses, creating good paying jobs that can't be exported. I'm Shelley Pingree, and I approve this message.

segments.text: Middle class families are paying the price for Washington's mistakes.
Wall Street gets bailouts, while we're stuck with record fuel prices, lost jobs, and rising health care costs.
In the state senate, I stood up to the special interests in big drug companies to fight for working families.
In Washington, I'll do the same, ending the corporate tax giveaways so we can start investing in main businesses,
creating good paying jobs that can't be exported.
I'm Shelley Pingree, and I approve this message.

01433.text: Times are tough, and America's off track. We're facing some real challenges, but together we can make sure Maine is ready to turn them into opportunities by investing in new sources of affordable energy that make Maine a leader in American-made fuels, and creating good-paying jobs that can't be exported. I'm Shelley Pingree, and I approve this message because I know we've got what it takes. Working with leaders from both parties, we can make the right choices, and finally put middle-class families first again.

01435.text: It was uncommon. Shelley Pingree had the courage to oppose the war in Iraq from the beginning, even when other Democrats supported George Bush. I stood up against the war and what it would cost our country. It's time to invest the $400 million we spend each day in Iraq here at home, on affordable health care, job creation, an education our kids can count on. I'm Shelley Pingree, and I approve this message because it's time to finally change Washington.

01436.text: As a new generation of our veterans come home, we're reminded of the debt we owe to all of these heroes. That's why I fought to secure new funding for veterans suffering from PTSD and make sure our retired vets receive their full pensions. And when Anthem Healthcare was using the fine print to deny veterans their claims at Togus, I said enough and we recovered $500,000 for their care. I'm Shelley Pingree and I approve this message because in Maine our veterans deserve our very best.

It's time to change Washington. I'm Shelley Pingree and I approve this message.

01438.text: I understand what small businesses mean to Maine. From the knitting company I founded to the restaurant my family and I now run, I know what it's like to pay taxes and meet a payroll. It's why Shelly is standing up to the special interest to put local businesses first. She fought to increase lending and cut taxes for small business so they can expand and grow. I'm Shelly Pingree and I approve this message because it's small businesses on Main Street, not Wall Street, that drive our local economy and keep Maine working.

I'm Shelly Pingree, and I approve this message.

Shelley Pingree, for Congress.

I'm Michelle Lujan Grisham and I approve this message. Because when America makes a promise, we should keep it.

Well, I'm not going to let that happen. If the President wants a fight on West Virginia coal, you'd better believe I'm going to give him one. I'm Shelley Moore Capito, and I approve this message. 01653.text: To be perfectly honest, I haven't made everyone in Washington happy. Like when I broke ranks with my party and voted to end tax breaks for oil companies. And I'm sure the President wasn't very happy when I voted to override his veto twice to make sure that our children get the health care they deserve. But I didn't go to Washington to make friends. I went to make a difference. I'm Shelly Moore Capito and I approve this message.

And I've just introduced a bill that mandates a dramatic increase in the production of clean coal to liquid fuel. Want to see OPEC sweat? It's time to show them the power of West Virginia coal. I'm Shelley Moore Capito, and I approve this message.

01655.text: Let me give you a quick briefing. Right now Cuba is seeking to drill for oil just 60 miles off the Florida coast. They're coming for our oil. My solution? I support America becoming energy independent by drilling off our shores. We force the big oil companies to spend more of their record profits on funding renewable energy sources. And to put an end to OPEX power, I've written a bill that uses West Virginia clean coal. Our economy and our security are at risk. It's time to play hardball. I'm Shelly Moore Capito and I approve this message. Thank you.

I'm Emily Cain and I approve this message because I'll always stand up for Maine's middle class.

Once Sherry got involved, things were done like that. Within a month, he had his pension back. Sherry Bustos cares about veterans. I'm Sherry Bustos, and I approve this message.

by ending the Bush tax cuts for millionaires so we can protect Social Security and Medicare,
and cutting off subsidies for big oil companies so we can preserve college funding and worker training.
I'm Suzanne Bonamici. I approve this message because Oregonians disapprove of Washington's priorities,
and it's time to set them straight.
Thank you.
